name: Stir-Fried Scallops with Pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. Granules (try to use the low-sodium kind such as Wylers). I use olive oil instead for a fresher taste. Thaw scallops if frozen.
2. Cook pasta according to package directions, EXCEPT omit oil; drain and keep warm.
3. Meanwhile, stir together wine, water, cornstarch, and chicken bouillon granules in a small bowl; set aside.
4. In a large skillet, cook garlic in hot margarine or butter for 30 seconds.
5. Add mushrooms, green onions, and carrot to the skillet; stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes or until the carrot is crisp-tender.
6. Stir in the sauce mixture.
7. Add the sauce, scallops, and parsley to the vegetable mixture in the skillet. Cook and stir until the sauce is thickened and bubbly.
8. Cook and stir for an additional 2 minutes or until the scallops are opaque.
9. Serve with lemon wedges.
10. Makes 4 servings (1 1/4 cups each). One serving equals: 2 lean meat exchange; 1 1/2 starch/bread exchange; 1 vegetable exchange; 1/2 fat exchange. Calories 247; Protein 23; Carbohydrate 26; Fat 4; Saturated fat 1; Cholesterol 37; sodium 340 (less if using low-sodium bouillon); Potassium 617.
